intro: Dortmanna xalapensis, also known as Xoconosostle or Wild Mexican Rhubarb, is a plant species native to Mexico that belongs to the family Grossulariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, gastrointestinal disorders, and as a natural laxative, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna xalapensis include tannins, flavonoids, and anthraquinones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used to prepare a decoction, which is a liquid extract made by boiling the plant material in water.
